bottlenecks.reason: Multiple nested loops iterate channel IDs and program lists (parsing + bulk DB payload construction). If channel/program counts are large this becomes O(N^2) and memory-heavy (building full in-memory payload before insert).
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Stream/stream-parse programs and insert per-channel in smaller batches; index programs by channel (map) to avoid nested iteration; perform heavy parsing in an isolate (already partly used) and avoid building giant in-memory maps before DB writes; use batch DB writes with controlled chunk sizes.

bottlenecks.reason: `LogoData.toJson()` includes the raw `bytes` field (Uint8List). Serializing raw image bytes into JSON (or in-memory maps) causes huge allocations and expensive serialization/deserialization.
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Avoid embedding raw bytes in JSON; store image files on disk and serialize only metadata + file path or a compressed representation. Perform any required conversions/compression in an isolate and stream/write to disk rather than keeping bytes in memory.

bottlenecks.reason: Building an indented JSON string for all SharedPreferences can allocate large contiguous memory and slow CPU (indentation increases size). Blocking the main isolate during conversion/writing causes UI jank for large prefs.
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Offload JSON encoding and file IO to a background isolate (use `compute`) or write a streaming/line-by-line export; avoid pretty/indented output for large backups and consider compressing backup files (gzip) to reduce memory/IO overhead.

bottlenecks.reason: Code calls `jsonDecode` / `jsonEncode` directly on SharedPreferences strings on the main isolate. If stored blobs grow large, decode/encode on the main thread blocks the UI and increases CPU & memory use.
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Use `compute(jsonDecode, data)` / `compute` for encode operations or move storage to a small local DB (sqflite) to avoid repeated large JSON serialization on the main thread. Limit the size of data saved in prefs.

bottlenecks.reason: Serializing entire playlist list into a single JSON string and writing to SharedPreferences is O(n) memory & CPU and can block the main isolate for large numbers of playlists.
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Persist playlists incrementally (per-playlist) or use a local database; offload serialization to an isolate; avoid storing very large JSON blobs in `SharedPreferences`.

bottlenecks.reason: Sequential network `head` requests inside a loop (`await _dio.head(pattern)`) cause serial network latency. For many channels this results in high overall latency and wasted time.
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Run network checks with controlled concurrency (e.g., a small worker pool / Future.wait with concurrency limit) and cache results. Abort early when a URL is found and progressively back off on failures.

bottlenecks.reason: Reading the entire backup file into memory and decoding as JSON blocks the main isolate if performed on the UI thread. Subsequent restoring loops write prefs sequentially, resulting in many awaited operations.
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Perform file read + JSON decode in an isolate (`compute`) and apply preferences in a batch or via a background worker; avoid awaiting per-key writes serially when possible (or write to a DB with transactional support).

bottlenecks.reason: There are blocking `Thread.sleep` / `SystemClock.sleep` calls in decompiled / third-party code under `tmp/` directories. If any such code is used at runtime or accidentally packaged, these are blocking and cause thread stalls.
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Remove unused decompiled files from the repository and ensure production code does not contain sleep-based busy waits. If delays are required, handle asynchronously or with non-blocking timers.

bottlenecks.reason: Mapping large in-memory maps/lists into JSON structures (calling `.toJson()` on many entries) can cause large temporary allocations and CPU usage when saving. Repeated full-map serializations are costly.
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Persist incremental changes rather than serializing entire maps each update, or offload serialization to an isolate; consider storing history in a local DB to avoid large JSON blobs in memory.

bottlenecks.reason: Bulk file system operations (delete recursive cache, recreate, reindex) can be expensive if cache is large; doing them synchronously in the main isolate without progress yields UI jank.
bottlenecks.recommended_fix: Perform heavy filesystem work in an isolate or background worker; show progress and use incremental deletion / pruning instead of full recursive deletes where possible.
